blocks.content:
In this milestone, you sign in to Grafana Cloud and navigate to Instrumentation Hub. Instrumentation Hub is your central control plane for discovering services and enabling zero-code instrumentation across your Kubernetes environment.

If Grafana Alloy isn't installed yet, you'll be prompted to install it as part of this step.
Sign in to your Grafana Cloud environment, for example `mystack.grafana.net`.
If you see the Alloy installation page, follow the on-screen steps to install Alloy. If Alloy is already installed, you'll see the main Instrumentation Hub page.
Verify you see tiles for **Application instrumentation** and **Kubernetes instrumentation**, along with a message showing how many services were discovered.
> **Note:** If you don't see discovered services, verify Alloy is running with `kubectl --namespace monitoring get daemonset` and check Alloy logs for connection errors.
In the next milestone, you'll view the discovered services and understand how they're organized.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Introduction to Grafana Alloy](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/introduction/)
- [Install Grafana Alloy on Kubernetes](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/set-up/install/kubernetes/)
